reviews.text: After months of procrastination and nervousness to have my testosterone checked, I finally made the decision to have it done. I seen their ad for a free consultation and check on the first visit and decided to give them a try. From the second I walked in the staff was super friendly and their pre visit registration process was super easy and quick. The blood work process was fast and got my results in minutes. Come to find out my levels were low and they were super helpful and answered all my questions and went over all treatment options without feeling pressured or rushed. Ended up signing up and did my first shot right there in the office! I'm so excited for my journey with gamedays men's health!

reviews.name: places/ChIJcZS1JiBjUocRkTPr9-Ksw2A/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25OVmFreGpka0ZPUjJwV09XSkZNM1ZwU1U1bmNWRRAB
reviews.text: Absolute SCAM! So unethical and honestly i question if it's even legal to do what they do here. Any place with a "membership fee" for healthcare is suspicious to begin with. This place is ridiculous! If they find your testosterone is low (spoiler alert: they probably will) they will ask for a $200 membership fee and then want you to pay $250 per month for meds. Mind you, they are prescribing these meds based off results of ONE blood draw. This is absolutely not normal protocol for prescribing testosterone replacement therapy. Good, responsible doctors will take at least two blood draws, which ideally would be early in the morning, and test a number of other levels rather than JUST testosterone. A males testosterone levels can fluctuate quite a bit throughout the day, and recommending an insanely expensive medication after ONE blood draw is not right . For reference, the average cost of testosterone replacement therapy (injections) for someone who has insurance is $30-$100 per month. If you decide to go here, be wary, and do yourself a favor- don't pay them anything until you get a second opinion.

reviews.name: places/ChIJcZS1JiBjUocRkTPr9-Ksw2A/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2paQ04yWm1aREJUWjNsUGJ6Y3pMV0ZFTmxsVFRuYxAB
reviews.text: Did their free testosterone check and was basically yelled at for trying to increase my levels naturally when I wouldn’t pay their ridiculous prices moving forward. Then was told I wasn’t allowed to take my results paperwork because “I wasn’t a member” pretty sure this is a violation of HIPAA but apparently the “medical professional” I was speaking to didn’t think so. But what do I know? Absolute waste of time to go here. All they do is try to sell you on their ridiculously high priced program without a care for you medically.

reviews.text:
I’m extremely picky with my health care providers, often to the point I’ll forgo treatment because finding competent providers who can also be trusted is a daunting task. Well that all came a head when I was no longer satisfied with my previous hormone clinic and I started shopping. After visiting other clinics with no real stand outs, I started asking my friends for recommendations. Routine was mentioned by a close friend, whom I figured if they were vouching for them I’d give them a try.

Routine Health did not disappoint. Despite being a new clinic, the staff is far from new. Each one has an extensive background in the hormone world. All my questions and concerns were all handled professionally and empathically. I got the sense they will do what’s best for my health and still work with me to reach my goals. reviews.text:
Vitalize Hormone has truly changed the trajectory of my healing journey.

Discovering that my progesterone was extremely low — and receiving the thoughtful, personalized guidance from Natalie on supplementation — has been absolutely life-changing. After starting my progesterone protocol, I’ve felt more balanced, grounded, and at home in my body than I have in a very long time.

I’m so grateful I found Vitalize Hormone when I moved to Utah. Everyone in the office is incredibly kind, attentive, and welcoming. From start to finish, I felt safe, supported, and genuinely cared for.

Natalie spends ample time walking through lab work in detail, explaining what needs attention and why. I left feeling informed, empowered, and hopeful — something I had been missing for a long time.

I’m looking forward to my follow-up to check in on my progress after implementing my new plan.
I truly couldn’t recommend Vitalize Hormone more!

reviews.text:
I've done extensive research on all TRT clinics in Salt Lake County and have also consulted with a urologist who adheres to older Medicare guidelines. In my search, only Revibe truly understood my unique situation.
​The difference in quality was stark. Unlike other clinics I visited, there were no high-pressure sales tactics. The physician assistant at Revibe demonstrated a level of understanding that surpassed any other physician I've spoken with on this topic. They answered all of my questions with confidence and clarity, which was a welcome contrast to the vague responses I received elsewhere.
​While you may pay slightly more at Revibe, the exceptional care and quality are immediately apparent. I cannot recommend them highly enough. If you live in Salt Lake County, they are an excellent choice, and I would even recommend working with them remotely if you are located elsewhere.

reviews.text: Dr Purser is a great doctor. He was able to help me when no one else did. Thank you, Dr Purser for your heart and expertise. His staff is also 5 stars, always helpful!

reviews.name: places/ChIJS-IBWkWETYcRrsdMYxNLreY/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tsVVFucDNlV0ptVFhkSVFUQTFNR1JoUTBaaVEzYxAB
reviews.text:
After more than four years of jumping from doctor to doctor with no real answers, I’m beyond grateful that I stumbled across Dr. Purser’s name in an MTHFR Facebook group. I had genetic testing done in the past and knew I had certain gene mutations that contributed to inflammation and other issues, but my health had declined significantly since the beginning of this year. The overall pain—especially at night—plus the constant numbness in my arms and hands was taking a huge toll on my quality of life.

I decided to give Dr. Purser a try and ordered the CNA with Redox bundle, along with a Zoom appointment. I was impressed even before speaking with him! About a week after my bloodwork, I received my results via email, followed by a detailed, easy-to-understand summary of my nutrient deficiencies. The plan included which vitamins to take, how often, and where to purchase them (none of which were products made by Dr. Purser, which I really appreciated).

Within a week of starting the supplements, I began noticing real improvement in my symptoms. By the time I met with Dr. Purser, I was already feeling hopeful again. He was kind, funny, extremely knowledgeable, and took the time to answer all of my questions. During the conversation, I found myself connecting the dots to past medical issues that now finally make sense.

After our appointment, I received a follow-up email outlining exactly what to continue based on my severe, moderate, and mild deficiencies. The level of detail and care from both Dr. Purser and his team is exceptional. For the first time in years, I feel like I’m on the right path toward healing. I can’t recommend him enough!

reviews.name: places/ChIJS-IBWkWETYcRrsdMYxNLreY/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tKbFdXRnVlR3hoZWt4VGFtTkRWSEZwV1VKVk1HYxAB
reviews.text: I had my first zoom meeting with Dr. Purser. He took his time with me. I didn’t feel rushed like I have in the past during traditional physician appointments. He was confident that he can restore my health and was informative in my bodies health deficiencies and how to correct them!! He gave me hope for the first time in a year!! I am excited to be on this journey and can’t wait to post about my restored health! I am appreciative for his knowledge and that he looks to find answers where no one else does. Thank you in advance.

reviews.name: places/ChIJAQBMvzqlTYcR1GVRVRlhvAM/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VLakh0ZFN3ak96dDBBRRAB
reviews.text:
I went to Revive Hormone Health a few weeks ago after it opened and signed onto its program.  I used to be more athletic, but I've slowed down, feel too tired. I put on a few pounds too. I thought something needs to change.

The staff at the clinic was really helpful, and made sure I got labs done so that they could figure out the best plan for me. The doc explained all the benefits and possible side effects of the treatments so that he and I could make the right choices about my health. Our plan has me following up with him on schedule. Like he says, he wants what's best, not what just seems best.

Since I started, I've seen nice improvements in my energy, mood and my overall performance. The therapy we decided on made a difference for sure. I feel like my old self again,  and with his follow-ups I've got my bases covered.  I recommend this place to anyone like me who just wants to feel like their old self again.

Ever since my first visit with Dr. Rick at the Madsen Health Center in the early 2010s, I knew he was an extremely special doctor. He packed so much advice and information into that short consultation. Clearly he was frustrated by the organization, and when I heard about Kestrel, I knew it was going to be perfect.

Thank you so much Dr. Rick from the bottom of my heart and from my whole family for helping me regain my health.

reviews.text: I have struggled with my weight my entire life. I went on my first diet when I was 10 years old, and yo-yo dieted my entire life. I could not sustain any weight loss know matter how I tried. I was a 50-year-old post-menopausal, 255-pound woman when I found Optimal Hormone Health. Working with the team there I had to face hard truths about my health and weight. They talked about ways to get my life under control and lose the weight. One option I took was weight loss medication. This medication was able to cure my insatiable hunger and that along with intermittent fasting helped me lose over 100 pounds. I’ve now kept the weight off over two years. I’m happier than I have ever been. I feel better than I have ever felt. I’m glad I did this for me! Now I will never hear again, “you’d be prettier if you just lost some weight”. The team at Optimal Hormone Heath is one of a kind and I can’t recommend them enough.

reviews.name: places/ChIJp0B8awz1UocRW8K_nz2HSbE/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tOSE5XTXhTRmc0T1VoS2EzZG5ZUzB5TW1kWU0zYxAB
reviews.text:
Dr. William Brant is a highly skilled and deeply respected urologist and sexual medicine specialist whose expertise and commitment to patient care are exceptional. With board certifications in both urology and sexual medicine, Dr. Brant brings a rare combination of surgical precision and a compassionate, holistic approach to men’s health. His extensive experience in treating complex conditions such as erectile dysfunction, Peyronie’s disease, and testosterone deficiency, along with his advanced surgical training, make him a trusted leader in his field.

Dr. Brant is known not only for his clinical excellence but also for his collaborative approach with mental health professionals, particularly in addressing the psychological and relational aspects of sexual health. His referrals are consistently thoughtful and thorough, and he takes great care to ensure that his patients receive integrated, multidisciplinary support. As a clinician, educator, and advocate for comprehensive sexual medicine, Dr. Brant exemplifies what it means to practice with both scientific rigor and genuine empathy.

I highly recommend Dr. Brant to patients seeking specialized care in urology and sexual health, as well as to fellow professionals looking for a trusted partner in collaborative treatment planning.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ41sRgsiIUocRjOkMrsbDwNo/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xsMlUybE9WRE16ZG1GYWJrdFlYMjFQTjNaamRXYxAB
reviews.text:
was very disappointed with my experience. I paid over $700 for two appointments, in addition to significant lab costs. For that price, I expected clear, well-reasoned recommendations and a thoughtful plan that fully incorporated the labs we had already drawn. I also expected to meet with a provider who was prepared and organized.

My first appointment was a consultation. I had sent my blood work in advance, but it was clear the provider had not reviewed it prior to the visit, as I had to pull everything up and walk through the results during the appointment myself. I assumed the second appointment would be better and confirmed with the office the day before that all labs had been received.

Unfortunately, the second visit was similarly disorganized. Much of the appointment was spent reviewing labs in a haphazard way, which gave me little confidence that they had been reviewed beforehand. One test that was run accidentally was unfamiliar to the provider, and I had to explain what it was based on my own research. I would have expected a provider to review and understand all test results prior to meeting with a patient.

Many of the recommendations felt speculative rather than evidence-based—suggestions to pursue hundreds of dollars more in testing despite no data pointing toward those conditions. Other recommendations consisted largely of supplements and cleanses that could conveniently be purchased through the office. When I asked about non-supplement alternatives (diet, lifestyle, etc.), those options felt secondary, and the overall approach seemed to default to taking a large number of pills rather than offering a clear, structured plan.

Additionally, when I mentioned that I am considering Accutane, I felt judged rather than supported. During my first appointment, I was also recommended a supplement that is known to negatively impact acne, despite acne being one of my primary concerns. In the second appointment, the provider doubled down on this recommendation, suggesting it might help acne, which is very unlikely according to available evidence and was directly contradicted by my dermatologist, who advised against it unless I have a severe deficiency.

Overall, the experience did not meet my expectations given the cost. I did not feel the care was sufficiently prepared, evidence-based, or personalized to my goals. That said, everyone there was nice. I just expect more from folks when I’m paying $700+

reviews.name: places/ChIJ41sRgsiIUocRjOkMrsbDwNo/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURCcnNXNzJ3RRAB
reviews.text:
I'm just starting my diagnosis and treatment with this group, but I want to share my journey with them to share cost > experience > process & plan of treatment.

Lyme  and co infections
Bacteria virus parasite

First- I’m not into hippie & I’m not into non proven medical practices, but I’m also  open to non traditional practices.
I’m not rich so a doctor outside of my insurance was a little scary.
Whatever I pay towards feels like a gamble.

I had great reviews for this office and both Dr. Peterson and Seibold.

I'm still taking my time to trust they can cure me but so far my experience’s been positive. Details of a few areas below.

1) customer experience:
Office manager Meghan/ Megan was soooo sweet & also soo knowledgeable. Even after my appt with the doc, she answered in-depth medication questions I expected the doc to only know. She wasn't B'sing either.

2) Blood work & check in
done by Vicki/Vicky. It was the smoothest & painless I've done. I usually pass out or get dizzy. She warmed a pad for me, chairs were comfy & she was so sweet.

3) Dr. Seibold
sat with me an entire hour! Ok that alone was such a valuable experience. someone care and listen.
I've felt so passed off by Dr. Berman at Uof U. but Seibold shared that even when he practiced in traditional medicine he would have passed me along too because that's how they are taught so they don't know other forms of treatment for bacterial, nuero, fungal viral etc. Seibold said he’s been practicing more whole body care integrative medicine for a few yrs but used to be a traditional nuts & bolts type doc.

Lyme journey
I've gone the traditional route
was referred by my GP at UofU for a lumber puncture & MRI even though I've been sick for so long. He saw the tick that bit me & still didn't order tests.

I may spend less here than what I'd pay for normal out of pocket limit with my insurance, which is 9k.

I think I could save money too if I lower my current premium with my insurance.; if these treatments work. I'm still at the testing but I'll report back to share info on future experiences.

Seibald shared science backed options that were non traditional and also uses traditional approaches for Lyme, such as antibiotics. Now these treatments aren't cheap but so far I'm thinking it will be less than my out of pocket limit.

They have things I didn't need like weight loss stuff but they didn't make me feel bad for not being into or needing it.
Just stay your course. They do a lot of stuff and don’t get sucked into any up sell unless you are financially fluid.

Insurance:
Most things are not covered by insurance, but they will try to help get tests covered if possible.

reviews.text:
I can't recommend Modern Male Clinic enough! From the moment I walked in, the staff, doctors, and nurses made me feel comfortable and well taken care of. The atmosphere is tailored perfectly for men it’s a relaxing, judgment-free zone where you never feel weird about addressing concerns about your body.

The procedure itself was smooth and efficient. They made sure I was comfortable the entire time, and the healing process has been a breeze thanks to their clear and easy-to-follow instructions.

On top of that, they really know how to treat their clients, plenty of snacks and drinks available, and they even ordered some amazing food for lunch! The pricing is fantastic too, not just for the hair transplant but for their other skincare products and procedures.

If you're a guy looking to improve yourself, this is the place to go. Don’t bother looking anywhere else, Modern Male Clinic has you covered!
